attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches final acceleration 50-100m from line, occasionally timing late surges perfectly when boxed in earlier. Not an attacker in traditional sense.
team_tactics: Relies on team positioning through final 5km but shows declining ability to maintain front position in technical or contested finishes. Bora leadout attempts noted but frequently fails to stay connected.
terrain_preference: Wide, uncomplicated roads with headwind finishes where tactical positioning trumps pure power. Struggles on technical finales requiring sustained positioning aggression through final kilometers.
vulnerability: Chronic positioning deficits in final 5km, especially technical finishes. Gets trapped far back in peloton tail. Dropped on flat stages before finale signals serious power deficit versus top-tier sprinters by 2019.
key_patterns: Caught far back (500m-5km) repeatedly despite team support, limiting contest ability. (4 observations)
Tactical veteran craft exploits headwinds and younger sprinters' timing errors when positioned correctly. (3 observations)
Physical decline evident 2017-2019: competitive when positioned well, but increasingly unable to maintain position. (5 observations)
Launching too early or aggressive positioning shifts suggest desperation tactics compensating for declining speed. (2 observations)
Boxed-in patience followed by late acceleration successful when achievable, but positioning failures prevent setup. (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: Clear decline 2017-2020. Still shows flashes of speed when positioned perfectly, but chronic inability to maintain front positioning and being dropped on flat stages indicate serious power deficit versus WorldTour sprinters
